rulings.Case Title: UNITY PACIFIC, INC. VS. HOWARD & 6TH STREET FOOD MARKET, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Amended Notice Of Motion For Order Converting Case To A General Civil Action And Granting Leave To File Its Second Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for February 11, 2025 line 1. PLAINTIFF UNITY PACIFIC, INC. AMENDED NOTICE OF MOTION FOR ORDER CONVERTING CASE TO A GENERAL CIVIL ACTION AND GRANTING LEAVE TO FILE ITS SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT is GRANTED. Plaintiff may filed its Second Amended Complaint. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: SUSAN LEE VS. NICOLE QUINTO ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for February 11, 2025 line 4. DEFENDANT NICOLE QUINTO, TOMMIE MOREAU DEMURRER to COMPLAINT is OVERRULED, 5 days to answer. Defendant asks the Court to sustain the demurrer without leave to amend in light of the pending rent board petition. Vargas v. Municipal Court (1978) 22 Cal.3d 902, cited by the Defendant, does not support such relief. The Court takes judicial Notice of the Rent Board Petition bearing a stamp "Received April 10, 2024" and of the notices of hearing for October 17, 2024 and January 2, 2025. There is nothing on the face of the complaint or in the judicially noticeable documents suggesting if and when a decision will be rendered on this petition that has been pending for 11 months. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
